lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Itzla. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Father should make a full recovery, though given that his would-be killers are still out there, that's not anywhere near as much comfort as it should be.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. Once I have, we can start planning our next move.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. However, in the meantime, I've devised a little plan for how we might go about dealing with our cultist problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We're going to go... undercover!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Undercover? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The Twilight Emissaries of course! If they are the ones responsible for what is now two attempts on my father's life, their deaths will be swift and brutal.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: However, given we're somewhat civilised people, we can't exactly start executing cultists without definitive proof. How do we get that? By joining up of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want us to join the cult?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Exactly! Quite a clever plan, if I do say so myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Prepare for the arrival of Queen Zyanyi Arkan!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah... This should be fun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd love to hear it, but I have some things I need to do first.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Very well. I'll be here when you're ready.

sections.title: Meeting with Queen Zyani Arkan to begin planning the infiltration
Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan after meeting with Queen Zyani Arkan

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. You should head on over to the pub in Quetzacalli Gorge. Remember, you'll need to rent the basement room. My contact will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head over there shortly.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It would seem I won't be able to join you on this mission, iknami. Still, I have no doubt you will be successful all the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Head to the pub in Quetzacalli Gorge and ask to rent the basement room. I have a contact who will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone you can trust?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: One of the only people left that I can trust. They'll fill you in on the details of how you'll infiltrate the Emissaries. Good luck, [player name]. May the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Mother.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: As I stand here, the healers toil to save the life of the Teokan of Ralos.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: He wished to return to the Teomat. I advised against it, but...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: So you would blame him?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Not exactly...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then take responsibility. You were charged with rooting out those who would plot against us. You failed. Why?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: With respect, progress has been made. We believe the Twilight Emissaries to be responsible, but we need to obtain proof before we can act.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We will infiltrate the cult and gather any information we can. Once we have confirmed their guilt, they will be eradicated.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: We?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This is a task of upmost importance. I intend to lead the infiltration myself.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: So you would chase glory like it is gold to be hoarded? You are the heir to Varlamore. It is not your place.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: In Varlamore we lead from the front!

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You do not lead at all, boy! You command the Knights of Varlamore. Use them!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I cannot trust them. We have been infiltrated and our enemies now sit beside us.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: You speak of Metzli?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It could be more than just her. One of my knights was implicated, but another may have incriminated him.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: There have been suspicions directed at Furia and Ennius.

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then all the more reason that you exclude yourself. Determine who you can trust and send them on this mission of yours.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: And if there is no one I can trust?

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Then you have failed as a leader.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Mother...

lines.speaker: Queen Zyanyi Arkan
lines.text: Now go. You have work to do. I don't expect you to fail me again.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, my Queen. May the sun light your way.

sections.title: Talking to the Bartender at Quetzacalli Gorge

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Welcome to the Windbreaker. We've got the best drinks around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to rent the basement room.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: We don't have a basement room... but I do have a nice big one here on the ground floor that's available. It's 30 coins for the night.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Like I said, we don't have a basement room. However, I do have a nice big one here on the ground floor that's available. It's 30 coins for the night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much on me right now.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Well you know where we are if that changes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take it.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Thank you very much. The room is all yours. Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's too much.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Well you know where we are if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Itzla again before fully going through the initial dialogue

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Itzla? Is that you? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. Any luck with that shopkeeper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Keep at it. He's our only way of infiltrating the Emissaries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guess I may as well have a little rest while waiting for this contact.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Rise and shine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Itzla? Is that you? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Isn't it obvious? I'm your contact!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... no?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Look, I fully appreciate what my mother said. I have carefully considered her instructions, but in this one very specific situation, I have come to the conclusion that they must be disregarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're the heir to the throne of Varlamore... What if someone recognises you?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well that's why I've covered my face of course. Simple!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This seems like a bad idea...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You may well be right, iknami, but that is a bridge we will cross, and then quite possibly burn, when we get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I can see you're still not convinced, so let me be frank. I wasn't lying when I spoke to mother. The number of people we can trust is concerningly low.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli, one of the most important people in the entire kingdom, has seemingly betrayed us. If your suspicions are right, Furia and Ennius are guilty as well. For all we know, there could be even more.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: At this point, the fewer people who know about our mission, the better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you trust me?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The irony is, my friend, that as an outsider, you're actually far more trustworthy than any Varlamorian now. You almost certainly can't be part of this conspiracy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm still not sure if I like this plan, but I see your point. What's our next move?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We need to infiltrate the Twilight Emissaries and confirm their guilt. I've done some digging, and rumour has it that they're on a bit of a recruitment spree at the moment. That's our way in.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The cult makes their home in the Twilight Temple, east of here. I suspect they won't take kindly to us just walking in, but the local shopkeeper here might be able to help us out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: He's been engaging in many a charitable undertaking recently. Food for the poor, potions for the sick, that sort of thing. You'd be mistaken for thinking him to actually be a reasonable person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he's not?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Sadly not in this case. The Emissaries accept members from all walks of life, but the vast majority of their recruits are those from more unfortunate backgrounds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah I see. They have people like this shopkeeper using charity to attract people to their cause.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Exactly. It is... unfortunate. The War of Betrayal was over a lifetime ago, but many still feel the impacts, especially in this part of the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'd like to think we have made great improvements over the years, but this is a clear reminder that far more needs to be done.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, it's not something to dwell on now. Let us get back to the more pressing matter of joining a dangerous cult.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. So this shopkeeper... If I go to him and pose as a potential recruit, your hope is that he'll contact the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Exactly! Return to me here once you've hopefully been successful and we can discuss the next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Queen didn't seem very happy with you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: That's parents for you. Do as I say, but not as I do. She was never afraid to get her own hands dirty. Did you know that before she was even my age, she became a champion in the Colosseum?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think she sees herself in you? If so, maybe she doesn't want you to make the mistakes that she once did?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I suspect you are right, but I would prefer to learn from my own mistakes. Every ruler must forge their own path. The people will never follow a leader who cannot stand alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose that makes sense, but I'm not sure how I feel about you learning from your mistakes while we infiltrate a dangerous cult together.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Fear not, iknami. I am confident that you will probably survive this mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good, I think...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you really think Metzli, Furia and Ennius are traitors?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli has always been an odd one. Everyone was always quick to dismiss it as just a little side effect of spending a lot of time with dead people. Now I wonder if she used that to her advantage.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: No one cared to pay close attention to her. No one wanted to spend much time around her. She probably got away with all sorts of things without anyone even noticing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But a traitor?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The evidence seems to suggest so. Why else would she have disappeared after the attack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe they captured her?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Maybe, but surely someone would have noticed. People generally don't go quietly when captured.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about Furia and Ennius?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well their family has of course produced traitors before, but they've never seemed to have much love for old Imafore.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: By the end of the war, I think he'd ended up killing more of his own family members than he left alive. Amazing what paranoia will do to a man.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Still, even if they never cared for him, his blood still runs in their veins, and they've never been short on ambition themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If they are guilty... what will you do to them?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The same thing we do to all traitors in Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens if they try to attack Servius again?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: He's in the capital now, where I wanted him to be from the start. He should be much safer there.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Still, safer is not safe, especially if we do have traitors in our midst. At any moment we risk someone showing some basic competence and actually managing to kill him properly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't consider the enemy to be competent?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: All I'm saying is that if I'd have been the one trying to assassinate my father, I'd probably have done a half- decent job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if the enemy isn't a threat, why are you here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: What do you mean, iknami?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If this isn't an enemy to be taken seriously, why do you feel you yourself need to be the one to deal with them? If they're incompetent, shouldn't anyone be able to handle them?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: So you challenge me, iknami? I'm wounded. However, as I said before, I do not feel we can trust many people while we have potential traitors around us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if they've managed to position some of their members in very important roles, surely they can't be completely incompetent?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We do not need to do this dance, [player name]. I understand that where you come from, there is a certain art to holding a conversation. We do not bother with that here. You may be direct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I just still don't know you that well. If we're about to infiltrate this cult together, I'd like to understand why you think it's important you do this yourself.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I see. I understand your doubts, my friend. I suppose I have a few reasons for wanting to be here.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: First, I do truly believe this mission has the highest chance of success if fewer people know about it. While I am not convinced on the competence of our foe, I still do not intend to underestimate them.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Second, I suppose I feel a certain level of responsibility. My father was attacked on my watch. When someone falters, it is their duty to fix it, especially when they hold a position like mine.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: After all, a leader who sits by and lets others deal with his mistakes is not someone to have faith in.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Finally, Servius is my father. Our relationship is not a simple one, but he is my father all the same. I would see to it personally that his attackers face justice.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I hope that satisfies you, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It does. Thank you, Itzla.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go and speak to this shopkeeper.

sections.title: Talking to the Quetzacalli Gorge Shopkeeper
Talking to him again after getting directions to the Twilight Temple

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Hello there. Looking for some supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the Emissaries...

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: I already told you to keep your voice down! Fetch your friend and then head east to the Twilight Temple. I will send word for them to expect you both.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks!

sections.title: Reporting back to Prince Itzla Arkan

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. Any luck with that shopkeeper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's going to reach out to the Emissaries and let them know to expect us at their temple.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuani! Let us not waste any time then. I will meet you at the Twilight Temple. You'll find it east of here, on the other side of the Proudspire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. I'll see you there shortly.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Hello there. Looking for some supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you can offer help to those in need.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Well I'm always happy to help those less fortunate than I, but you don't really look like you need that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if I should be insulted by that, or flattered... But anyway, you misunderstand. I'm not looking for help myself. Instead I was hoping to do some helping alongside you.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Thank you for the offer, but I'm not really looking for help right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? I do also quite like death.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, end of the world, holy fire, final dawns, that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Are you a fool? Keep your voice down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I'm not here to cause problems. I just want to help.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: You want to join the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And a friend as well.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: You know there are tests you'd need to pass, right? Not everyone can be considered worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine by me. I love tests!

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: There is a penalty for failing them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, not quite as fun, but I'm still up for it.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Well... if you're sure, fetch your friend and then head east to the Twilight Temple. I will send word for them to expect you both.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lovely! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We still haven't been called upon yet. We may as well mingle with these other potential recruits while we wait.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Nova
lines.text: Greetings, iknami. It's good to see so many who are willing to contribute to the Final Dawn. The word of the Augur has changed everything. The time to act is now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Augur?

lines.speaker: Nova
lines.text: You have not heard? She is the one who now leads the Emissaries. She has taken this once helpless collective and turned them into the ones who will guide us all to Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know all this? You're not even a member yet.

lines.speaker: Nova
lines.text: I was once. In my time of need, I turned to the Emissaries, but they were even more helpless than I was.

lines.speaker: Nova
lines.text: Now though, she has changed everything. Now it is my duty to rejoin their ranks so that we may bring about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Caritta
lines.text: Hello! You're not from around here, are you? It's exciting to see that even people from outside of Varlamore are looking to join the Emissaries!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well after I heard about all the good work they were doing, I could hardly say no! What about you? What made you want to join them?

lines.speaker: Caritta
lines.text: Well it's our duty of course! The Church of Ralos has become lost, but the Emissaries know the truth. Ximoua will not come to be unless we make it so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're quite eager to see the end of the world then?

lines.speaker: Caritta
lines.text: End? Ximoua is no end! It is the beginning! We will all come together as one and bring about a new world! I can't wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad to see you're so excited. Good luck with the tests.

lines.speaker: Caritta
lines.text: And you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Felius
lines.text: Leave me be. I am not here to make friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Sergius
lines.text: I'm not sure I should be here. Maybe all this was a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Sergius
lines.text: I want to help... I want to believe... I really do! It's just all so intimidating...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure it will be fine.

lines.speaker: Sergius
lines.text: I hope so.

sections.title: Reporting back to Prince Itzla Arkan

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a chat to some of the other recruits. One of them had something rather interesting to say.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently, the Emissaries gained a new leader recently. They refer to her as the Augur.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess there's only one way to find out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... Did you learn anything else of note?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently this Augur will lead the Emissaries to Mokhaiotl. Do you know what that means?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Mokhaiotl? I'm not my father, so you'll have to forgive my knowledge on this sort of thing, but if I remember right, Mokhaiotl plays a key role in Ximoua.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remind me what Ximoua is?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It is said that one day, Ralos and Ranul will come together, uniting all souls as one. In doing so, they will bring about the destruction of this world, but also the birth of a new one.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Most accept that we cannot predict when this day will come. However, our friends in the Twilight Emissaries don't seem happy with that. They instead believe in something called Tsak Tauli, the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I remember. They see it as their duty to make Ximoua happen. So where does this Mokhaiotl fit in?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: When Ralos and Ranul come together, it is said they will do so at Mokhaiotl, the centre of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Given that the Emissaries see it as their duty to bring about the end, I suppose it makes sense that they'd want to reach Mokhaiotl themselves.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well I'd love to know how they plan on reaching the centre of the world. I can't imagine it's the kind of place that you can just stroll on down to.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Itzlan. [player name]. It is time for you to prove your worth. Enter the tower when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Pick up your follower and then let us head inside and see what tests await us.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Let us head in and see what tests await us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It is said that Ralos and Ranul will come together at Mokhaiotl, the centre of the world. It is there that they will unite all souls as one, destroying this world and creating a new one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Given that the Emissaries see it as their duty to bring about the end, I suppose it makes sense that they'd want to reach Mokhaiotl themselves.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well I'd love to know how they plan on reaching the centre of the world. I can't imagine it's the kind of place that you can just stroll on down to.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Itzlan. [player name]. It is time for you to prove your worth. Enter the tower when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Pick up your follower and then let us head inside and see what tests await us.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: But it seems we will have to ponder this mystery later. Let us head in and see what tests await us.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you, er...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Itzlan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Itzlan?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Itzlan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... So what happens now that we're here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It seems this tower is where the Emissaries evaluate possible new members. If we're to get anywhere here, we're going to need to pass whatever tests they might have for us.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Apparently, someone called Forebearer Janus will call upon us when it's our turn to be tested. In the meantime, we may as well mingle with these other potential recruits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think they might know something of note?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Unlikely, but it would be interesting to know why they woke up one day and decided their calling in life was to join a cult.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This whole place is very fancy.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, I must admit the Emissaries have a more elaborate setup than expected. They've never been this organised before, nor have their numbers been so great. Something must have changed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did they build everything here themselves?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Thankfully they are not quite that well put together. The temple here was built not long before the War of Betrayal. It is said that Imafore wanted somewhere dedicated to Ranul that would rival the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm guessing he was too foolish to realise that the Teomat is equally dedicated to both Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Anyway, the temple was considered an affront to both the Church of Ralos and the Sect of Ranul. After Imafore died, most people were more than happy to see it abandoned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the Emissaries eventually moved in?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, but only very recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... I wonder what the cause was.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: If all goes well, we will hopefully soon find out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We still haven't been called upon yet. We may as well mingle with these other potential recruits while we wait.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get mingling.

sections.title: Descending into the Tower of Ascension
Attempting to climb up before speaking to Janus

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Itzlan. [player name]. When you are ready to begin, climb the stairs. Your first trial awaits.

sections.title: Speaking to Forebearer Janus in the lobby after restarting or failing some trials

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: You have trials to complete, [player name]. Climb up the stairs when you are ready.

sections.title: Attempting to open the chest in the lobby without permission

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Hey! You haven't been given permission to open that!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Hey you! You're not allowed up there.

sections.title: Unlocking the south-west gate and chest
Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan before receiving the poem

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: These emissaries here are acting a bit strange... They keep putting their hands in their pockets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We should keep looking for that passphrase.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Found anything of note, iknami?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this book that might be of use.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... Seems some of the words are highlighted. I doubt that's a coincidence.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan after reading the poem

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you could help me translate some words?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Translate? Well I guess I can give it a go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Silam.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, I recognise this language. It's the language of the Old Ones. Servius did teach me a few of their words, though I may not have paid as much attention as he'd have liked...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This one means... 'West'!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! Could you translate another?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I can try.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Silam? I think that means 'west'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makt.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Makt? I believe that means...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: 'North'! It means 'north'.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Makt? I think that means 'north'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uitt.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Uitt? If I remember correctly, that translates to 'south'.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Uitt? I think that means 'south'.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Uitt? I believe that means...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: 'South'! It means 'south'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Takam.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, I recognise this language. It's the language of the Old Ones. Servius did teach me a few of their words, though I may not have paid as much attention as he'd have liked...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: This one means... 'East'!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Takam? If I remember correctly, that translates to 'east'.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Takam? I think that means 'east'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I'm done translating for now.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well I hope it helps, iknami.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan with the Completed note

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I might have the passphrase.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuani! You'd better let Forebearer Janus know.

sections.title: Talking to Forebearer Janus with the Completed note

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Recruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that passphrase...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Do you have it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Moki Koma Ikam.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: No, that's not quite right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'd better keep searching.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: That is correct. Well done! You and your companion may progress to the next trial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite ready.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Speak to me when you are ready to start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Welcome to your next trial. Here, there is one simple goal...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Survive.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Speak to me when you are ready to start.

sections.title: Talking to an Emissary Ascended before completing the First Trial

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: I don't know if you'll ever figure this one out!

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Struggling? I've seen a lot of people fail to work this one out.

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Do you think you've got the intellect to complete this one?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Welcome to your first trial. The aim is simple: Speak the correct pass phrase to me and you will be allowed to proceed. Let the trial begin!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Recruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that passphrase...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Do you have it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite yet, but I'll have it in no time.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: We shall see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are we meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: It's quite simple: You just need to work out the passphrase and say it to me. You may use whatever you have available in this room to discover it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to an Emissary Ascended after completing the First Trial

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: I admit, that was pretty fast. Still, it will take more than that for you to prove your worth.

sections.title: Talking to Forebearer Janus after completing the first trial

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Are you ready to start the next trial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite ready.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Then come back to me when you are.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Recruit. Are you ready to start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me what this trial is?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: The goal is simple: Survive. That is the only advice I can offer you.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Now, are you ready to begin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not quite ready yet.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Speak to me again when you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: As am I.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Then let the trial commence.

sections.title: Talking to Itzla before beginning the fight

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It seems some of the boxes have been left around the edges of the room...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll check them out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I think it's time for a fight! This one should be fun!

sections.title: If Itzla dies during the fight

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: It appears your companion has fallen in battle!

sections.title: Restarting the trial after Itzla dies

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: An unfortunate fate for your companion on your last attempt. Do you want to try again?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Great work there, [player name]. I wonder what our next trial will be.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: You have fought well... This trial is now complete!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Recruit. Any luck finding the traitor yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Remember, only one of the four before you is guilty of disloyalty. Question all four and determine who it is. I suggest you choose carefully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what's our plan here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: My guess is that one of them has some beliefs that don't quite line up with those of the Emissaries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we question them about that sort of thing? I can't imagine they'd be willing to be truthful about that.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: They can't be truthful about something they don't know about. Take advantage of gaps in their knowledge - gaps that a true Emissary wouldn't have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, it sounds like they plan to kill whoever is guilty.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Shouldn't have joined a crazy murder cult then. Or if they really were committed to joining a crazy murder cult, they should have at least made sure they were actually good at it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not even have a little bit of sympathy for them?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I guess I'd better get questioning.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: How's it going, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we know about the Emissaries and their beliefs?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Probably not as much as we should, but what we do know is that the Emissaries believe in Tsak Tauli, more commonly known as the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Like with Ximoua, the Final Dawn sees Ralos and Ranul come together, uniting all souls as one. In doing so they destroy this world and create a new one.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: After that, the two gods separate again, but the souls they united stay within them. The cycle then starts over once more.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The Emissaries seem to consider it their singular purpose to bring about the Final Dawn. Nothing else matters to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information. Hopefully it will help me work out who this traitor is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still have some questioning to do.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Good luck!

sections.title: Talking to Janus during the trial

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Recruit. Any luck finding the traitor yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Remember, only one of the four before you is guilty of disloyalty. Question all four and determine who it is. I suggest you choose carefully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! Time for some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you join the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Tenoch
lines.text: To do my duty, of course. Ralos and Ranul wish us to bring about the end of this world. Only then will we see the birth of a new one.

lines.speaker: Tenoch
lines.text: To do my duty, of course. Ralos and Ranul wish us to bring about the end of this world. Only then will we will all unite in Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you worship?

lines.speaker: Tenoch
lines.text: I worship both Ralos and Ranul equally, for in reality they are one. They have been separated for now, but soon they will rejoin once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Tenoch
lines.text: The Final Dawn is our destiny. Ralos and Ranul will unite us all and end this world, but in doing so, we will all play a part in forming a new one.

lines.speaker: Tenoch
lines.text: The Final Dawn is our destiny. Ralos and Ranul will unite us all and end this world, but in doing so, they will grant us eternal life in Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! Time for some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you join the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Silia
lines.text: I always knew people were wrong about me. I knew I would serve a higher purpose. I never cared what it was. I only cared that in doing so, I would be serving the will of Ralos.

lines.speaker: Silia
lines.text: I always knew people were wrong about me. I knew I would serve a higher purpose. I never cared what it was. I only cared that in doing so, I would be granted a place in Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you worship?

lines.speaker: Silia
lines.text: I have served Ralos my whole life. I am his humble servant. It was he who guided me here so that I may serve his will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Silia
lines.text: The Final Dawn is an end, but also a beginning. It is when Ralos will use his holy fire to destroy this world so that he and Ranul can build a new one.

lines.speaker: Silia
lines.text: The Final Dawn is an end, but also a beginning. It is when Ralos will use his holy fire to destroy this world. Then he and Ranul will guide us to Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! Time for some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you join the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Adrius
lines.text: To be honest, I wasn't too sure about joining at first. My friend convinced me though. Here we're part of something bigger than ourselves.

lines.speaker: Adrius
lines.text: To be honest, I wasn't too sure about joining at first. My friend convinced me though. Here we have a chance to be granted a place in Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you worship?

lines.speaker: Adrius
lines.text: I've never been one for proper worship, but I believe in the teachings of Ralos and Ranul. I hope those teachings guide me through whatever is to come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Adrius
lines.text: They say the Final Dawn is inevitable. It's the end that we can't escape from. It's a scary thought, but the Twilight Emissaries have embraced it, so I have as well.

lines.speaker: Adrius
lines.text: They say the Final Dawn is inevitable. It's the end that we can't escape from. It's a scary thought, but at least here I have a chance to control my destiny and obtain a place in Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! Time for some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you join the Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Eleuia
lines.text: I wished to see my god in mortal form and serve his will. In return for my service, the Emissaries promised me they could grant that wish.

lines.speaker: Eleuia
lines.text: I wished to see my god in mortal form and be granted eternity in Mictl. In return for my service, the Emissaries promised me they could grant that wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do you worship?

lines.speaker: Eleuia
lines.text: I am a humble servant of Ranul. It has always been my hope to stand before him and be judged as worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Eleuia
lines.text: The Final Dawn is the will of Ranul. He will show us all that death is nothing to be feared as he and Ralos guide us into forming a new world.

lines.speaker: Eleuia
lines.text: The Final Dawn is the will of Ranul. He will show us all that death is nothing to be feared. He and Ralos will lead us into forming a new world before then guiding us to Mictl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I choose [member's name]. This is your traitor.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: I'm afraid you have made the wrong choice. Let's try this again...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Nicely done. [member's name] was indeed the traitor. You have both done well, but I wonder how well you will work together in the final trial...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Speak to me when you are ready to proceed.

sections.title: Restarting the trial after a false accusation

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Be sure to choose more carefully this time, recruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

sections.title: Choosing a member after the traitor has been killed

lines.speaker: [Innocent member]
lines.text: Hey! No need for that. You found the failure among us already.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan after the execution

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Great work, [player name]. On to the final trial!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Are you ready to start the next trial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Welcome to your third trial. Before you stand some recently initiated acolytes. However, it seems one of them was not as loyal to our cause as first believed.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Your task is to question all four and determine who the traitor is. I suggest you choose carefully. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite ready.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Then come back to me when you are.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Are you ready to start the next trial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Well done, recruits. Victory is within your reach, but one more trial awaits...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Face your opponent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: An Emissary must be prepared to sacrifice anything and everything. Only through sacrifice will we deliver Twilight's Promise. Only through sacrifice will we achieve the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well, you heard the man, [player name]. Shall we dance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if you should be so...

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Ready yourselves! May the best warrior win!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite ready.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Then come back to me when you are.

sections.title: During the fight against Prince Itzla

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You might want to dodge this one!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Reckon you can dodge this?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'd move if I were you!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Prayers? What prayers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not allowed to do that!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I yield!

sections.title: Speaking to Forebearer Janus after defeating Prince Itzla

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: You and your companion will fit in nicely here. Let us head to the main chamber to discuss what happens next.

sections.title: Attempting to head back up the stairs

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: You don't need to be going up there. You've already completed your trials.

sections.title: Speaking to Prince Itzla in the lobby

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well that was fun!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know you're not meant to be enjoying yourself, right?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Don't be like that! Surely you must admit you enjoyed it a bit as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I liked the part where I beat you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Right, moving swiftly on. Let's see what Forebearer Janus has to say to us.

sections.title: Speaking to Forebearer Janus in the lobby

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Congratulations to you both on completing the trials. You will make fine Emissaries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Now the Augur herself will oversee your initiation. You may collect your new robes from that chest over there. Put them on and proceed into the temple.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla after talking to Forebearer Janus

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We'd better change into those robes.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla after getting the robes

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Let's head over to the temple.

sections.title: Talking to Forebearer Janus after getting the robes

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: You should proceed to the temple. The Augur awaits.

sections.title: Searching the chest without enough inventory space
Reclaiming the robes if they're lost
Searching the chest with the robes
Attempting to enter initiation without the robes on

lines.speaker: Emissary Ascended
lines.text: Hey! You don't have permission to go in there!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Itzlan and [player name], I take it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's us.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Good. Allow me to welcome you to the Tower of Ascension. You are here because you hope to be welcomed into our family. We hope to welcome you as well, but alas, not all can be deemed worthy.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: Here, in the Tower of Ascension, you will demonstrate your intellect, strength, loyalty and ability to sacrifice. In doing so, you will prove your worth to Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Janus
lines.text: When you are ready to begin, climb the stairs. Your first trial awaits.

sections.title: Talking to Fides before Itzla after the initiaion

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Welcome, acolyte, and congratulations on your initiation. I am just making the final preparations on the duties for you and your companion. They will be ready shortly.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla after the initiation
Talking to Itzla again before Fides

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Let's see what duties this Forebearer Fides has for us.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well that was a level of insanity that even I wasn't ready for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They think Ennius and Furia are Ralos and Ranul reborn...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Like I say, complete insanity! Metzli has twisted the minds of everyone here. They eagerly lap up her outlandish claims without ever stopping to consider how ludicrous they are.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: In some ways, I pity them, but alas, there is only one cure for this sort of thing. This cult must be eradicated, especially Metzli, Furia and Ennius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still confused over what their plan is... They want to reach Mokhaiotl, but why would they need to kill Servius to do that? And why the whole set up with that Knight of Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: What do you mean, iknami?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When Furia and Ennius set that knight up, it was so obvious. It's like they wanted us to know it was them.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm... A very good point. Could be this cult has just addled their brains, but it may be that they wanted us here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A trap?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I guess we will find out soon enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should we not leave? We know the cult is guilty now, and we know Metzli, Furia and Ennius are part of it. We have everything we need.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You said yourself, there are questions still unanswered. We must rectify that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if there is a trap, you'd willingly spring it?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well it would be the last thing they'd expect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh boy...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Let's have a chat to this Forebearer Fides about our new duties. Whatever they are, hopefully they give us a chance to learn more.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Welcome back, acolytes. You should head below and support the efforts in opening the path into Tapoyauik. Do this, and you will be rewarded by Ralos and Ranul themselves!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We'll get right to it!

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla after Fides

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Let's head down into these ruins.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Welcome, acolytes. Congratulations on your initiation.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Thank you! We're absolutely ecstatic to be here and we're ready to serve!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: I am glad to see you are so eager. We have an essential task for you to help perform. The completion of this task may well be essential in us finding a route to Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you have us do?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: After the Augur was blessed with glorious purpose, she felt a calling to this place. She came to this fallen temple and built us a home. However, it was not the temple that called to her, but what lies below.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Below?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: This temple was built atop Tapoyauik, a ruined fortress of the Old Ones. They walked the world during the last cycle, and when the time came for their cycle to end, they embraced their destiny willingly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Willingly?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Of course! They basked in the glory of Ralos and Ranul and united with them as one, but before they did, they left behind the secrets needed to ensure that we too could fulfil our purpose.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: And the Augur? She found those secrets below?

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: She did, but there is more to be uncovered. That is where you come in. Tapoyauik is a hostile environment and the passage into the ruins has been blocked.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Head below and support the efforts in opening the path once more. Do this, and you will be rewarded by Ralos and Ranul themselves!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We'll get right to it!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: How are you with a pickaxe, iknami? I must confess, my own skills with one are rather poor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did they never teach you how to do a bit of hard labour back when you were young?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: We all have different callings, my friend. Mining just wasn't mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clearly combat wasn't either given that performance earlier.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oh I see! You win one fight and you start thinking you're something special.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes actually.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well then a bit of mining shouldn't be any issue for you then. Get to it!

lines.speaker: Forebearer Vulcan
lines.text: Excellent work! Ralos and Ranul have blessed us on this day!

lines.speaker: Emissary Acolyte
lines.text: What's going on?!

lines.speaker: Emissary Acolyte
lines.text: Nagua! Run!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Itzla! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well... I'm alive, which I must be honest, I wasn't really expecting after that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't see any way over to you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nor can I. I think we're just going to have to press on and hope that we can meet up again further ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Do you see any other options?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess not.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Than I shall meet you up ahead. Good luck!

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla by the frozen door

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: There you are! I was starting to worry you'd got lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There were a few obstacles in my way.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well unfortunately it seems we have another before us. I can't see any way to open this door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I'll have a look around and see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Any luck with this door?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I ask you about some words I found carved into the wall?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oma.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oma? If I remember right, that means the number 'two'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeya.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yeya? That means...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I think it means 'three'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Naui.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Naui? Hmm...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't know what that one means.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not to worry. I'm sure I can still work it out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zema.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Zema? Let's see... That could mean a few things. 'Together', 'people' and 'one' are the ones I remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think Metzli found down here?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nothing is my guess. The whole point of Ximoua is that it can't be, and shouldn't be, predicted. It's meant to teach us to live freely and not worry about that which we can't control.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So she's lying?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: That, or she herself was so desperate to find a greater meaning in something, that she misinterpreted what she did find.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I suppose it doesn't matter though. This Final Dawn business is total nonsense, but if the Emissaries believe in it, then we need to work out what they consider so important about this place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On that note, I guess I should get back to the door.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Indeed. There must be some way to open it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing yet.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: There must be some way to open it...

sections.title: If Prince Itzla targets an ice nagua

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Help me kill this thing, would you?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Great work opening the door, [player name]. Time to see what awaits us on the other side.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: Welcome, friends. You chosen few stand here because you have proven yourselves to be worthy. Here, you will not just find a home, you will find a family. Together, this family will bring about the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Forebearer Fides
lines.text: No doubt you are wondering how we will achieve our destiny. I call upon our glorious leader, the Augur, to explain our holy mission.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: This morning, you were nothing more than ants, scrambling to find purpose amongst the dirt. Now though... now you have achieved ascension.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Once, I was just the same. Like so many of you, I started my life with nothing. I would wake each morning and pray that I would find enough food to last through the day.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: All the while, those above me treated me worse than a common criminal. I was a villain to them! Why? Simply because they were born with privilege, and I was not.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Eventually, I looked to the Sect of Ranul for guidance. I thought they might have shown me my path, but I was wrong.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Before long, I was their leader and it was instead me who was being looked to for guidance, for purpose. In truth though, even then I had nothing I could offer. I was as blind as I had always been.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: But the day came when I finally saw my purpose, for I was blessed with a vision, granted to me by Ralos and Ranul themselves! I will share now with you what they shared with me.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Thirty one years ago, the moon eclipsed the sun and a false night fell across the land. That day marked a beginning, for it saw the birth of a pair of twins.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: To most, they were children like any other, but I was shown the truth of their birth, for as the sun and moon combined, Ralos and Ranul were born in mortal forms!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Now, they seek Mokhaiotl. There, they will unite us all as one, and a new world will be born. My purpose, which I now share with you, is to lead them there.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Blasphemy! Deceit! That is what people cry when they hear the truth. The Church of Ralos teaches that our work is false, and our royal leaders lap up their every word!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Perhaps you still harbour doubts yourselves. I do not judge you. We have all been lied to our entire lives, and in truth, even after I was blessed by our gods, I held many doubts myself.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: But you have all been chosen. You have all achieved ascension. I know that you are among the few who will understand. You are among the few who can see the truth!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: And see it you will! You do not need to take the word of a mere herald. Instead, bask in the glory of our gods reborn!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: You may recognise these faces as Ennius and Furia Tullus, but now I give you the truth. Before you stand our gods! Ralos and Ranul!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Together, we will accompany them on their glorious journey! We will guide them to Mokhaiotl and bring about the Final Dawn! This is our purpose! This is our destiny! This is Twilight's Promise!

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: You all have a part to play in what is to come. Forebearer Fides will assign you your duties. Speak with her and then go forth. Go forth and serve our gods!

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: Zema ami miki!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get to Fortis and let them know what happened!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get over to Fortis rather than going in there again.

sections.title: Attempting to climb the tower again
Talking to Servius, Teokan of Ralos

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Ah, nilsal, my child. It is good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Servius? I wasn't expecting you to be up so soon.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Ralos still has need of me, my child. I will not bow down to a mere knife wound.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad to see you're doing well, but I'm afraid I have some bad news. Itzla has been captured!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Captured? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We infiltrated the Twilight Emissaries together. We discovered Metzli to be their new leader, with Ennius and Furia at her side. She's claiming the two of them are Ralos and Ranul reborn!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Blasphemy and betrayal in equal measure! They will all burn in the light of Ralos for this! And you say they have captured the Prince as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They have. I don't know where they've taken him.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: This is dire news. I must speak to the Queen at once. Is there anything else I should be aware of before doing so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cult are apparently trying to reach Mokhaiotl.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Mokhaiotl is not a place that can be reached! These fools are truly lost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know... Something isn't right about all of this. We encountered a nagua in some Old One ruins. She called Mokhaiotl the Tlati archive.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: You should be careful of trusting the words of a nagua. Still, I must seek the guidance of Ralos on this matter. First though, I will speak with the Queen.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: I would suggest you make yourself scarce. The Queen will not take kindly to this development. I would hate for you to face her fire, my child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I want to help.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: And help you will. I will ensure you are involved in whatever plans are made for dealing with this cult, but for now, you should leave this to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thank you, Servius.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Timoiva, my child, and may the sun light your way.

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: Humans... You stand before Amoxliatl. State your intent.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oh... Hello there.

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: A greeting is not a statement of intent. State your intent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Well we're looking for information. The cultists in the temple above apparently found something down here. We need to know what they found so we can stop them.

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: Your statement lacks specificity. Clarify. What information?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: On Mokhaiotl. The cultists are trying to get there.

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: Mokhaiotl? The Tlati archive? That place is not for your kind. You misused the gifts you were granted. You are unworthy.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Tlati? As in the rainforest? What do you mean? And what gifts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! Where's Itzla?

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: Itzla? He is no longer your concern.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Allow me to deal with this nuisance, Augur. The fire of Ralos is within me. He can be the first to bask in it.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Save your fury, brother. This one is unworthy of it.

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: Human arrogance. Here in Tapoyauik, I decide who is worthy.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Silence, nagua! Do you not know who stands before you?

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: You? You are nothing. You defile this place. Now face the consequences.

lines.speaker: Augur Metzli
lines.text: There is nothing more for us here. If the nagua wants blood, our friend here will provide plenty...

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: You have been abandoned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get after them!

lines.speaker: Amoxliatl
lines.text: After them? No. You will not be going anywhere...
